steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Calculate angle L in radians by multiplying angle K by 15
steps.content:
First, simplify angle K:
$$K = \frac{\pi}{2 \cdot 15} = \frac{\pi}{30} \text{ radians}$$
Then multiply by 15 to find angle L:
$$L = 15 \cdot K = 15 \cdot \frac{\pi}{30} = \frac{15\pi}{30} = \frac{\pi}{2} \text{ radians}$$

steps.title: Convert from radians to degrees
steps.content:
Use the conversion factor: $\pi$ radians $= 180^{\circ}$
$$L = \frac{\pi}{2} \text{ radians} = \frac{180^{\circ}}{2} = 90^{\circ}$$
That matches Choice D — $90$.
